title: senecio adnivalis var. alticola
intro: Senecio adnivalis var. alticola, also known as Alpine Groundsel, is a rare and lesser-known plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally used in herbal medicine, this plant is reported to have medicinal properties due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Alpine Groundsel has been used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive problems and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Senecio adnivalis var. alticola can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
